human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for administrative support and business-services quality specificity, source quality, hold and release boundary discipline, nonconformance and CAPA handling, incident escalation, functionality coverage, deployment notes, commercialization notes, and specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative support and business-services quality evidence review, hold and release posture, nonconformance handling, CAPA escalation, service-organization control evidence, and quality findings summaries.

Authority sources cover NAICS/BLS industry scope, ISO quality/service/complaint/audit workflows, GAO and NARA evidence controls, AICPA service-organization controls, NIST privacy/access/audit controls, and OSHA safety incident escalation.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, incomplete evidence, release pressure during a hold, repeat defects, safety incident escalation, closure without review support, overbroad industry interpretation, adjacent HR routing, low-confidence handling, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and service-organization evidence gaps.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/administrative-support-and-business-services/business-services-quality-specialist/ and remains distinct from administrative operations, compliance, HR, finance, legal, safety, security, records, facilities, and formal audit lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around hold or release overrides, closure without review support, formal audit or certification claims, HR placement decisions, safety-sensitive incidents, and client-contract interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated spec pack for business-services quality and assurance handling in administrative support and business services.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
